commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a code-mode exec `command` alias, hook-visible `toolKind` and `toolInputKind` metadata, alias reconciliation, docs, and focused regression coverage for outer code-mode exec hooks.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. A trusted policy that returns only `command` or changes `language` before a normal hook runs will flow through `policyAdjustedParams` without re-normalized aliases or recomputed metadata.
commands.review_followup: There is one narrow, actionable repair: preserve the new code-mode exec hook contract after trusted-policy rewrites and cover that path.

target.title: fix(code-mode): align outer exec hook params
target.body:
Follow-up to #83387 after #83481 landed the active hook-context plumbing.

## Problem

Code-mode exposes its outer `exec` control tool through the Pi custom-tool adapter. On current `main`, the active hook context is already threaded into that outer dispatch via `toolHookContext`, which gives `before_tool_call` handlers the active agent/session/run identity.

The remaining gap is that the outer code-mode `exec` input is model-facing `code`, while conventional exec-shaped hook policies inspect `command`. That makes it harder for one policy to reason consistently about outer code-mode `exec` and normal exec-shaped dispatches.

## Change and value

This PR preserves the current active hook-context path and aligns the outer code-mode `exec` input contract with exec-shaped policy expectations:

- keeps `code` accepted as the documented model-facing field
- accepts `command` as an alias for code-mode `exec`
- mirrors `code` and `command` in `before_tool_call` hook payloads for marked code-mode control tools only
- adds hook-visible `toolKind: "code_mode_exec"` and `toolInputKind` metadata so policies can distinguish code-mode cells from shell-style `exec`
- reconciles single-alias hook rewrites before execution
- rejects divergent `code` and `command` values before execution
- treats non-string counterpart values as absent so hooks still see executable source under both expected names

## Scope

Affected:
- code-mode users with `before_tool_call` hooks or trusted policies that inspect outer code-mode `exec`
- policy authors who want outer code-mode `exec` to expose an exec-compatible `command` field while still being distinguishable from shell exec

Not affected:
- normal shell `exec` behavior, which remains unmarked by the code-mode discriminator
- non-code-mode tools named `exec`
- the existing code-mode `code` field, which remains accepted
- nested tool calls made from inside code mode; the discriminator applies only to the outer code-mode `exec` control tool

## Verification

Current head: `e8763b3685a3a547353fea717d62978fc57b03ea`.

- Local: `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check docs/plugins/hooks.md docs/reference/code-mode.md src/agents/code-mode-control-tools.ts src/agents/code-mode.ts src/agents/pi-tool-definition-adapter.ts src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.ts src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.integration.e2e.test.ts src/plugins/hook-types.ts`
- Local: `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- Local: `timeout 120s node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.integration.e2e.test.ts -t "code-mode exec|plain exec|single-alias" --reporter verbose` passed: 4 tests passed, 14 skipped
- Local runtime hook proof: `timeout 60s node --import tsx /tmp/openclaw-code-mode-hook-proof.mjs`
- GitHub CI on this head: passed (`gh pr checks 83483 --repo openclaw/openclaw --watch --interval 15`)

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Outer code-mode `exec` now exposes executable source under both the model-facing `code` field and the exec-shaped `command` alias when `before_tool_call` hooks inspect the dispatch. The same outer dispatch keeps the active agent/session/run/tool-call context from the #83481 hook-context path and includes `toolKind: "code_mode_exec"` plus `toolInputKind` so shell-style exec policies can deliberately distinguish code-mode cells.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw source worktree at head `e8763b3685a3a547353fea717d62978fc57b03ea`, Node v26.1.0, pnpm 11.1.0, Linux x64. The runtime proof registered a real global `before_tool_call` hook runner, sent the code-mode `exec` control tool through the production Pi custom-tool adapter, and compared it with an unmarked plain `exec` tool.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** From the PR worktree, ran the selected before-tool-call integration tests that construct production code-mode tools with `createCodeModeTools()`, pass the outer `exec` through the Pi custom-tool adapter, exercise wrapped and unwrapped hook paths, verify single-alias rewrites, and compare marked code-mode `exec` against an unmarked plain `exec` tool:

```console
$ timeout 120s node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.integration.e2e.test.ts -t "code-mode exec|plain exec|single-alias" --reporter verbose
```

Then ran a throwaway runtime hook proof that registered a real global hook runner and captured redacted event/context payloads from both code-mode `exec` and an unmarked plain `exec`:

```console
$ timeout 60s node --import tsx /tmp/openclaw-code-mode-hook-proof.mjs
```

**Evidence after fix:** The selected regression run completed successfully:

```console
✓ src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.integration.e2e.test.ts > before_tool_call hook deduplication (#15502) > passes agent context to outer code-mode exec hooks through Pi custom tools 29ms
✓ src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.integration.e2e.test.ts > before_tool_call hook deduplication (#15502) > marks code-mode exec without marking plain exec hooks 5ms
✓ src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.integration.e2e.test.ts > before_tool_call hook deduplication (#15502) > normalizes outer code-mode exec hook params when a wrapper owns the hook 5ms
✓ src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.integration.e2e.test.ts > before_tool_call hook deduplication (#15502) > mirrors single-alias hook rewrites for code-mode exec aliases 2ms

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  4 passed | 14 skipped (18)
```

The runtime hook proof produced this redacted live hook observation:

```json
{
  "proof": "OpenClaw runtime before_tool_call hook observation",
  "head": "e8763b3685a3a547353fea717d62978fc57b03ea",
  "codeModeResult": {
    "status": "blocked",
    "deniedReason": "plugin-before-tool-call",
    "reason": "runtime proof captured code_mode_exec"
  },
  "plainExecResult": {
    "status": "blocked",
    "deniedReason": "plugin-before-tool-call",
    "reason": "runtime proof captured plain_exec"
  },
  "observations": [
    {
      "event": {
        "toolName": "exec",
        "toolKind": "code_mode_exec",
        "toolInputKind": "javascript",
        "runId": "run-runtime-proof",
        "toolCallId": "proof-code-mode-exec",
        "params": {
          "hasCode": true,
          "hasCommand": true,
          "codeEqualsCommand": true,
          "code": "console.log('runtime proof');",
          "command": "console.log('runtime proof');",
          "language": "javascript"
        }
      },
      "context": {
        "toolName": "exec",
        "toolKind": "code_mode_exec",
        "toolInputKind": "javascript",
        "agentId": "main",
        "sessionKey": "agent:main:runtime-proof",
        "sessionId": "session-runtime-proof",
        "runId": "run-runtime-proof",
        "toolCallId": "proof-code-mode-exec"
      }
    },
    {
      "event": {
        "toolName": "exec",
        "toolKind": null,
        "toolInputKind": null,
        "runId": "run-runtime-proof",
        "toolCallId": "proof-plain-exec",
        "params": {
          "hasCode": false,
          "hasCommand": true,
          "codeEqualsCommand": false,
          "command": "echo runtime-proof"
        }
      },
      "context": {
        "toolName": "exec",
        "toolKind": null,
        "toolInputKind": null,
        "agentId": "main",
        "sessionKey": "agent:main:runtime-proof",
        "sessionId": "session-runtime-proof",
        "runId": "run-runtime-proof",
        "toolCallId": "proof-plain-exec"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

**Observed result after fix:** Hooks inspecting outer code-mode `exec` see `code` and `command` mirrored for code-only, command-only, and malformed-counterpart inputs. Those same hook events and contexts include `toolKind: "code_mode_exec"` and `toolInputKind: "javascript"` for the outer code-mode control tool. A plain unmarked tool named `exec` still reaches hooks without `toolKind`, so existing shell-style policies can distinguish the two paths instead of relying on tool name alone. A hook rewrite that returns only `command` is reconciled before execution.

**What was not tested:** I did not run a live model/gateway conversation. The runtime hook proof intentionally blocked before executing source so the evidence isolates the `before_tool_call` contract and does not depend on model output.
target.author: kaspre

commands.reason: author association CONTRIBUTOR and repository permission none are not allowed
